2605.19908.title: Where Does Authorship Signal Emerge in Encoder-Based Language Models?
2605.19908.paper_summary: Authorship attribution models fine-tuned with the same pretrained encoder, data, and loss can differ four-fold in performance depending only on their scoring mechanism. We use mechanistic interpretability tools to explain this gap. Stylistic features such as word length, punctuation density, and function-word frequency are equally available at every layer in every model, including in an off-the-shelf control encoder, hence the gap not coming from representation quality. Instead, causal intervention shows that the scorer determines where the encoder consolidates authorship signal. Mean pooling forces consolidation by early to mid layers, while late interaction defers it to later layers. We further derive this difference from the gradient structure of each scorer, and training dynamics reveal distinct learning trajectories that follow from that difference.
2605.19908.paper_summary_zh: 作者歸屬模型在相同的預訓練編碼器、數據和損失下，根據其評分機制的不同，性能可以相差四倍。我們使用機械解釋工具來解釋這一差距。風格特徵如單詞長度、標點密度和功能詞頻率在每個模型的每一層都是同樣可用的，包括在現成的控制編碼器中，因此這一差距並不來自於表示質量。相反，因果介入顯示評分器決定了編碼器整合作者信號的位置。均值池化強迫在早期到中期層進行整合，而晚期交互則將其推遲到後期層。我們進一步從每個評分器的梯度結構中推導出這一差異，訓練動態揭示出由於這一差異而產生的不同學習軌跡。
2605.19908.author: Francis Kulumba et.al.
2605.19908.authors: Francis Kulumba, Guillaume Vimont, Laurent Romary, Florian Cafiero
